idea-plugin.name: MCP Server Companion
idea-plugin.description:
MCP Server Companion extends the built-in JetBrains MCP Server with additional tools for AI clients such as Claude, ChatPGT, Cursor...

Requires: the JetBrains MCP Server plugin to be installed and enabled. It is bundled in IntelliJ IDEA Ultimate 2025.1+.

Source code and documentation: github.com/maximehamm/mcp-intellij-all

Settings

Each tool can be individually enabled or disabled in Settings → Tools → MCP Server Companion.

Available tools

Editor & Navigation

  • get_open_editors — Open files, focused editor with caret position, and current text selection.
  • get_file_problems — IDE-detected errors, warnings, and infos for a file or all open editors.
  • get_quick_fixes — Quick fix suggestions at a specific file:line:column. Use after get_file_problems to get actionable fixes for a reported problem.
  • navigate_to — Opens a file and places the cursor at a given line and column.
  • select_text — Opens a file and selects a text range (ready to copy with Cmd+C).
  • highlight_text — Highlights multiple zones in a file using the IDE's search-result color (theme-aware). Press Escape to clear.
  • clear_highlights — Removes all highlights added by highlight_text.

Build & Tests

  • get_build_output — Build tool window: structured error/warning tree with file names and line numbers, plus raw console text.
  • get_console_output — Console output from both the Run and Debug tool windows. All tabs from each window, with the active window and active tab clearly indicated.
  • get_services_output — All sessions from the Services tool window, grouped by node name: SQL output log, result grids with column names, and which session/tab is currently active.
  • get_test_results — Last test run results: passed/failed/ignored status, duration, and failure messages with stack traces.

Debug

  • debug_run_configuration — Launches a run configuration in debug mode.
  • get_debug_variables — Local variables and their values from the current debugger stack frame (available when paused at a breakpoint).
  • get_breakpoints — Lists all line breakpoints with file, line number, enabled state, and condition expression.
  • add_conditional_breakpoint — Adds a breakpoint with an optional condition expression.
  • set_breakpoint_condition — Sets or removes a condition on an existing breakpoint.
  • mute_breakpoints — Mutes or unmutes all breakpoints in the active debug session.

Diagnostic & Processes

  • get_ide_settings — Read IntelliJ IDE settings: Gradle build delegation, SDK, compiler, encoding. Pass a keyword (search) to filter, a key for direct lookup, or a prefix (with optional depth) to retrieve all settings in a subtree.
  • get_intellij_diagnostic — One-call diagnostic: indexing status, active notifications, running/paused processes, and idea.log WARN/ERROR tail. Use this first when something is not working as expected.
  • get_running_processes — Lists all active and paused background processes (indexing, Gradle syncs, builds, etc.).
  • manage_process — Pauses, resumes, or cancels a background process by title.

General

  • get_mcp_companion_overview — Describes all available MCP Companion tools and how to use them.
  • get_project_structure — Project SDK, modules, source roots, excluded folders, and module dependencies. All paths relative to project root.
  • refresh_project — Syncs the project build system (Gradle or Maven). Detects the build system automatically from the project root.
  • execute_ide_action — Executes any IntelliJ action by ID (e.g. ShowSettings, ReformatCode, GotoFile), or searches for action IDs by keyword.
  • replace_text_undoable — Replaces text in a file via IntelliJ's document API (supports Cmd+Z undo).
  • delete_file — Deletes a file from the project (undoable).

Example prompts

  • "Add a breakpoint at line 18, start the debugger, stop when i == 3, then tell me the value of jj."
  • "Check my build output and fix the compilation errors. Make changes undoable."
  • "Run the tests and tell me which ones failed and why."
  • "Open Main.java and highlight all usages of variable i."
  • "Something is wrong in IntelliJ — run a diagnostic and tell me what you see."
